ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Nyɛ tsɛ nɛ ngɛ hiɔwe ɔ le kaa ni nɛ ɔmɛ tsuo a he ngɛ nyɛ hiae.
translated: I want to go to where he is to eat it and be well.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Se mo o lɛɛ, ke o ngɛ kpakpa pee ɔ, ko ha o muɔ nine nɛ le nɔ nɛ o hiɛ ɔ nine peɔ.
translated: If you say so, and you eat the porridge, but the children with them are not there to see it.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Nɛ mɛ he je nɛ nyɛ peɔ yeyeeye ngɛ tade he? Nyɛ susu nga nɔ mɔmɔ ɔmɛ a he, bɔ nɛ a puɛɛ ɔ: atsu we ni, jeha nɔ hu a tsɛ̃ we kpa.
translated: What did you do to me that was so bad? It's not right what you said to me, but you didn't eat Atsu's child, the one who didn't eat our food.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Nɛ Yesu ba nɛ e kɛ mɛ ba tu munyu ke: “A ngɔ he blɔ tsuo ngɔ ha mi ngɛ hiɔwe kɛ zugba a nɔ.
translated: And Jesus said to them: "If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move."

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Nɛ e fa asafo ke a hi si ngɛ nga a nɔ. Nɛ e kukuɔ abolo enuɔ kɛ lo enyɔ ɔ, nɛ e hyɛ hiɔwe nɛ e gbaa nɔ, nɛ e kuɔ mi, nɛ e ngɔ ha e kaseli ɔmɛ, nɛ e kaseli ɔmɛ ngɔ ha asafo ɔ.
translated: And he went to the assembly when they saw that he was not there. And he ate the remaining food, and he went to Hiowe and took nothing, and he died, and he came to his town, and his town came to the assembly.
